title: Open Game Selector and see all supported generations
description: Verifies the /play page loads and shows a grid of available Pokémon games for Generations 1–5.

title: Select Gen 1 and reach the Gen 1 team builder
description: Verifies clicking the Gen 1 game card routes to the Gen 1 team builder page.

title: Select Gen 2 and reach the Gen 2 team builder
description: Verifies clicking the Gen 2 game card routes to the Gen 2 team builder page.

title: Select Gen 3 and reach the Gen 3 team builder
description: Verifies clicking the Gen 3 game card routes to the Gen 3 team builder page.

title: Select Gen 4 and reach the Gen 4 team builder
description: Verifies clicking the Gen 4 game card routes to the Gen 4 team builder page.

title: Select Gen 5 and reach the Gen 5 team builder
description: Verifies clicking the Gen 5 game card routes to the Gen 5 team builder page.

title: Return to game selector from the team builder via in-app navigation
description: Verifies the user can get back to the game selection screen using visible navigation controls after entering a team builder page.

steps.description: Click on "Play" or "Change Game" navigation option

title: Add a Pokémon to the first available team slot via search and click
description: Verifies searching filters the selection list and clicking a Pokémon adds it to an empty team slot.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click into the Pokémon search bar
Click on the "Pikachu" Pokémon card in the results list
Verify text "Pikachu" is visible in the team panel

title: Toggle dex mode to National and filter by version 'Red'
description: Verifies dex mode toggle and version filter update the available Pokémon selection list.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click the dex mode toggle to switch from Regional to National
Verify text "National" is visible near the dex mode control
Select "Red" from the version filter dropdown
Verify element "Pokémon selection list" is visible

title: Remove a Pokémon from a team slot using the remove button
description: Verifies a Pokémon can be removed from the team and the slot returns to an empty state.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click into the Pokémon search bar
Click on the "Pikachu" Pokémon card in the results list
Click the remove button on the team slot containing "Pikachu"
Verify text "Pikachu" is not visible in the team panel

title: Undo and redo a team change using on-screen controls
description: Verifies undo reverses the last team edit and redo restores it.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click into the Pokémon search bar
Click on the "Pikachu" Pokémon card in the results list
Verify text "Pikachu" is not visible in the team panel
Verify text "Pikachu" is visible in the team panel

title: Defensive coverage heatmap renders after adding a Pokémon (Gen 1)
description: Verifies the defensive type coverage heatmap appears once at least one team member is added.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 (Gen 1) game option
Click on "Add Pokémon" (or equivalent team slot add button)
Click on the first Pokémon result in the picker/list
Verify element "defensive coverage heatmap" is visible

title: Heatmap shows all 18 type labels when rendered
description: Verifies the heatmap includes the full set of type labels once it is visible.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 (Gen 1) game option
Click on "Add Pokémon" (or equivalent team slot add button)
Click on the first Pokémon result in the picker/list
Verify element "defensive coverage heatmap" is visible

title: Empty team shows prompt and does not render defensive coverage
description: Verifies that with an empty team, the heatmap is hidden and the prompt message is shown.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 (Gen 1) game option
Verify text "Add a Pokemon to view defensive coverage" is visible
Verify text "Defensive Coverage" is not visible

title: Heatmap appears after transitioning from empty team state to having 1 Pokémon
description: Verifies the UI switches from the empty-state prompt to the heatmap after adding a Pokémon.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 (Gen 1) game option
Verify text "Add a Pokemon to view defensive coverage" is visible
Click on "Add Pokémon" (or equivalent team slot add button)
Click on the first Pokémon result in the picker/list
Verify element "defensive coverage heatmap" is visible
Verify text "Add a Pokemon to view defensive coverage" is not visible

title: Heatmap disappears and returns to prompt after removing the only Pokémon
description: Verifies defensive coverage is hidden again when the last Pokémon is removed from the team.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 (Gen 1) game option
Click on "Add Pokémon" (or equivalent team slot add button)
Click on the first Pokémon result in the picker/list
Verify element "defensive coverage heatmap" is visible
Click on "Remove" (or the delete/clear control) for the added Pokémon
Verify text "Add a Pokemon to view defensive coverage" is visible
Verify element "defensive coverage heatmap" is not visible

title: Gen 1 offensive coverage is shown after adding multiple Pokémon
description: Verifies the offensive type coverage panel displays super-effective coverage after adding multiple Pokémon to a Gen 1 team.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Click on a second Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team

title: Empty team shows the offensive coverage empty-state message
description: Verifies the offensive coverage area prompts the user to add Pokémon when the team is empty.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Verify text "Add Pokemon to show offensive coverage" is visible

title: Offensive coverage updates when a new Pokémon is added
description: Verifies the offensive coverage panel changes after adding an additional Pokémon to an existing team.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Click on a second Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Verify text "Add Pokemon to show offensive coverage" is not visible

title: Removing a Pokémon returns offensive coverage to empty-state when team becomes empty
description: Verifies the offensive coverage panel returns to the empty message when the last Pokémon is removed from the team.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Click on the added Pokémon in the team area to open its actions
Verify text "Add Pokemon to show offensive coverage" is visible

title: Offensive coverage panel remains visible and readable after scrolling
description: Verifies the offensive coverage section can be reached and is visible after scrolling on the team builder page.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Verify text "Add Pokemon to show offensive coverage" is visible

title: Offensive coverage distinguishes between STAB and non-STAB (if both are displayed)
description: Verifies the offensive coverage panel shows labels or sections indicating STAB and non-STAB coverage when available in the UI.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Click on a second Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team

title: Offensive coverage shows a list or visualization of types covered
description: Verifies that after adding Pokémon, the offensive coverage panel shows type names or a visual representation of covered types.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Click on a second Pokémon in the available Pokémon list to add it to the team
Verify element "Type coverage visualization" is visible

title: Smart Picks shows a ranked list with scores after building a partial team
description: Verifies that opening Team Recommendations after adding team members displays a visible ranked list of suggestions with scores.

steps.description: Click on the Gen 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon in the Gen 1 Pokédex/list to add it to the team
Click on a second Pokémon to add it to the team
Verify at least one recommended Pokémon entry with a visible score is shown

title: Clicking a recommended Pokémon adds it to the first available team slot
description: Verifies that selecting a recommendation adds that Pokémon to an empty slot on the team.

steps.description: Click on the Gen 1 game option
Verify at least one recommended Pokémon entry is visible
Click on the first recommended Pokémon in the list
Verify the selected Pokémon name is visible in the team slots area
Verify the recommendations panel remains usable or closes without an error message

title: Smart Picks can add a recommendation when the team already has some members
description: Verifies that with a partially filled team, clicking a recommendation adds to the next open slot without replacing existing members.

steps.description: Click on the Gen 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team
Click on a second Pokémon to add it to the team
Click on a recommended Pokémon entry
Verify the team now shows 3 Pokémon filled (the two original plus the newly added one)

title: Team full: clicking a recommendation shows a replace/cancel prompt or a Team full notice
description: Verifies that when all 6 team slots are filled, selecting a recommended Pokémon does not silently overwrite and instead shows a visible prompt/notice.

steps.description: Click on the Gen 1 game option
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team (slot 1)
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team (slot 2)
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team (slot 3)
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team (slot 4)
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team (slot 5)
Click on a Pokémon to add it to the team (slot 6)

title: Opening Smart Picks with an empty team still shows recommendations (or a helpful empty-state)
description: Verifies that the recommendations panel handles an empty team gracefully by showing suggestions or a clear instructional empty-state.

steps.description: Click on the Gen 1 game option
Verify the recommendations panel is visible
Verify either a list of recommended Pokémon is visible or an empty-state message explaining to add team members is visible

title: Load a gym boss preset and view populated opponent team and matchup matrix
description: Verifies a gym preset can be selected and the opponent team plus matchup matrix render with populated content.

steps.description: Select a gym preset from the preset list

title: Switch realism mode from Sandbox to Strict and confirm predictions update
description: Verifies toggling realism changes the displayed matchup predictions according to game rules.

steps.description: Select a gym preset from the preset list

title: Run matchup analysis and view win/loss predictions and recommended assignments
description: Verifies the Analyze action produces visible win/loss predictions and recommended slot assignments.

steps.description: Select a gym preset from the preset list

title: Analyze with no opponent selected shows validation error
description: Verifies the user cannot analyze without selecting an opponent team or preset and sees the correct validation message.

steps.description: Verify text "Select an opponent team or preset before analysis" is visible

title: Open preset picker and confirm gym/Elite Four/Champion categories are available
description: Verifies the boss preset picker exposes the expected opponent categories for selection.

title: Create a manual opponent team and confirm matchup matrix renders
description: Verifies a user can choose to create a manual opponent team (instead of a preset) and see a matchup matrix section for analysis.

title: Save a custom opponent team for reuse and verify it appears in saved list
description: Verifies a custom opponent team can be saved and then becomes available for reuse in the battle planner.

steps.description: Click "Save" in the opponent team section

title: Capture Guide shows capture locations for each team Pokémon after selecting a version
description: Verifies the Capture Guide panel lists capture locations for each team Pokémon when a supported game version is selected.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on "Capture Guide" to open the Capture Guide panel
Select a game version from the version filter
Verify text "Capture location unknown" is not visible

title: Capture Guide shows placeholder when selected version lacks location data for a team Pokémon
description: Verifies the Capture Guide displays the placeholder message for any Pokémon without location data in the selected version.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on "Capture Guide" to open the Capture Guide panel
Select a version that is known to have incomplete location data (if multiple are available, pick an alternative version from the current selection)
Verify text "Capture location unknown" is visible

title: Changing version filter updates the Capture Guide results
description: Verifies switching the version filter changes the capture guide output on the page.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on "Capture Guide" to open the Capture Guide panel
Select a game version from the version filter
Verify text "Capture location unknown" is not visible
Select a different game version from the version filter
Verify text "Capture location unknown" is visible

title: Capture Guide panel can be opened and closed without losing the selected version
description: Verifies the Capture Guide remains consistent after closing and reopening, keeping the selected version filter value.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on "Capture Guide" to open the Capture Guide panel
Select a game version from the version filter
Click on "Capture Guide" to close the Capture Guide panel
Verify text "Capture Guide" is not visible
Click on "Capture Guide" to open the Capture Guide panel

title: Capture Guide stays usable after switching away to another tab/panel and returning
description: Verifies the user can move to another team-builder panel and come back to Capture Guide with content still visible.

steps.description: Click on the Generation 1 game option
Click on "Capture Guide" to open the Capture Guide panel
Select a game version from the version filter
Click on "Type Coverage" (or another visible team analysis tab/panel)
Click on "Capture Guide" to return to the Capture Guide panel

title: Ask a strategy question and receive an AI response in the conversation
description: Verifies the AI Coach panel can send a chat question and display the assistant response in the conversation thread.

steps.description: Click on "AI Coach" (or "Coach") to open the AI Coach panel
Type "Who should I lead with and why?" into the message input field
Verify text "Who should I lead with and why?" is visible in the conversation
Verify an assistant reply message is visible in the conversation

title: AI Coach panel loads prior messages when opened
description: Verifies previously stored AI messages (if any) are displayed when the panel is opened.

steps.description: Click on "AI Coach" (or "Coach") to open the AI Coach panel

title: Request team analysis and view structured results plus usage indicator
description: Verifies the Analyze Team action generates a structured analysis and the usage quota indicator is visible/updated.

steps.description: Click on "AI Coach" (or "Coach") to open the AI Coach panel
Verify element "Analyze team" button is visible
Verify text "Analysis" is visible in the AI Coach panel
Verify structured analysis content is visible (for example "Strengths" or "Weaknesses" or "Type coverage")
Verify a usage quota indicator is visible (for example "Usage" or "quota" or "remaining")

title: Usage quota indicator is visible and persists while interacting with the panel
description: Verifies that the usage/quota indicator is shown and remains visible after sending a message.

steps.description: Click on "AI Coach" (or "Coach") to open the AI Coach panel
Verify a usage quota indicator is visible (for example "Usage" or "quota" or "remaining")
Type "Any quick tips for improving type coverage?" into the message input field
Verify an assistant reply message is visible in the conversation
Verify a usage quota indicator is visible (for example "Usage" or "quota" or "remaining")

title: Prevent sending an empty message
description: Verifies the UI blocks sending when the message input is empty.

steps.description: Click on "AI Coach" (or "Coach") to open the AI Coach panel
Click into the message input field
Verify text "Required" or "Message cannot be empty" is visible

title: Show a visible loading state while waiting for an AI reply
description: Verifies a loading indicator appears after Send and before the assistant response is shown.

steps.description: Click on "AI Coach" (or "Coach") to open the AI Coach panel
Type "Explain my team’s biggest weaknesses." into the message input field
Verify text "Thinking" or "Loading" is visible in the AI Coach panel
Verify an assistant reply message is visible in the conversation

title: Save a team after signing in when prompted from the builder
description: Verifies a user can initiate Save Team from the builder, sign in when prompted, then save successfully and see the team appear in the Saved Teams panel.

steps.description: Verify element "Enter team name" is visible
Type "Gen1 Smoke Team" into the team name field

title: Saved teams list is visible and non-empty after saving a team
description: Verifies that after a successful save, the Saved Teams panel shows the newly saved team name in the list.

steps.description: Click the "Gen 1" option (or a "Gen 1" link) to open the Gen 1 team builder
Type "Panel Visibility Team" into the team name field
Verify text "Panel Visibility Team" is visible

title: Attempt to save with empty team name shows validation and does not save
description: Verifies the Save Team confirmation enforces a team name and blocks saving when the name is empty.

steps.description: Click the "Gen 1" option (or a "Gen 1" link) to open the Gen 1 team builder

title: Delete a saved team with confirmation removes it from the list
description: Verifies a user can delete a saved team from the Saved Teams panel after confirming, and it no longer appears in the list.

steps.description: Click the "Gen 1" option (or a "Gen 1" link) to open the Gen 1 team builder
Type "Delete Me Team" into the team name field
Verify text "Delete Me Team" is visible
Click the "Delete" action for "Delete Me Team"
Verify text "Are you sure" is visible
Verify text "Delete Me Team" is not visible

title: View My Teams dashboard with teams grouped by generation
description: Verifies an authenticated user can open My Teams and see teams displayed in generation groupings with team card details.

steps.description: Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email or username field
Click the "My Teams" link in the site navigation

title: My Teams dashboard shows team cards with sprites and type badges
description: Verifies team cards on the dashboard visibly include Pokémon sprites and type badge information.

steps.description: Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email or username field
Click the "My Teams" link in the site navigation
Verify element "a team card" is visible
Verify element "a Pokémon sprite image within a team card" is visible

title: Open a saved team in the builder from a team card
description: Verifies that clicking Open in Builder navigates to the builder and loads the selected team.

steps.description: Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email or username field
Click the "My Teams" link in the site navigation
Click the "Open in Builder" button on the first visible team card

title: Cancel deletion at the confirmation step
description: Verifies that starting a delete flow and cancelling does not remove the team from the list.

steps.description: Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email or username field
Click the "My Teams" link in the site navigation
Click the "Delete" button on the first visible team card

title: Delete a team using two-click confirmation
description: Verifies a user can delete a team via the two-step confirmation and sees it removed from the dashboard list.

steps.description: Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email or username field
Click the "My Teams" link in the site navigation
Click the "Delete" button on the first visible team card
Click the "Confirm" button in the delete confirmation

title: My Teams dashboard handles empty state (no saved teams)
description: Verifies that when a user has no saved teams, the dashboard shows a helpful empty-state message instead of broken UI.

steps.description: Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email or username field
Click the "My Teams" link in the site navigation

title: View a public trainer profile and core identity details
description: Verifies a public profile page shows avatar, bio, and member-since information for an existing user.

title: View favorites section on a public trainer profile
description: Verifies the favorites area shows favorite games and favorite Pokémon for an existing user.

steps.description: Verify element "favorite games list" is visible
Verify element "favorite Pokémon list" is visible

title: Browse the list of public saved teams on a trainer profile
description: Verifies the profile displays a visible list of public saved teams that can be browsed.

steps.description: Verify element "public teams list" is visible

title: View the featured team section on a public trainer profile
description: Verifies a featured team is shown when the user has one set and it is visible on the page.

steps.description: Verify element "featured team preview" is visible

title: Nonexistent trainer profile shows Profile not found
description: Verifies an unknown username results in a not-found state with a clear message.

steps.description: Verify text "Profile not found" is visible
Verify element "trainer avatar" is not visible

title: Profile page shows stable layout while data loads (no error state)
description: Verifies the profile page renders without showing a not-found message for an existing user, even while content loads.

steps.description: Verify text "Profile not found" is not visible
Verify element "favorite games list" is visible
Verify element "public teams list" is visible

title: Update profile successfully (username, bio, avatar, favorites) and see confirmation
description: Verifies an authenticated user can edit profile fields and save changes with a visible success confirmation.

steps.description: Type "Building a Gen 1–5 team." into the "Bio" field
Verify element "Bio character counter" is visible
Verify element "Avatar frame options" is visible
Click on a game in "Favorite games"
Click on another game in "Favorite games"
Click on a Pokémon in "Favorite Pokémon"
Click on another Pokémon in "Favorite Pokémon"

title: Prevent save when username is taken and show inline error
description: Verifies real-time username validation blocks saving when the username is unavailable and shows an inline error.

steps.description: Verify text "Please choose a different username" is visible

title: Bio character counter updates as user types
description: Verifies the bio field shows a visible character counter that changes when text is entered.

steps.description: Verify element "Bio character counter" is visible

title: Favorite games selection enforces max of 5
description: Verifies the user can select favorite games up to the maximum and the UI prevents selecting more than 5.

steps.description: Click on five different games in "Favorite games"
Click on a sixth game in "Favorite games"

title: Favorite Pokémon selection enforces max of 8
description: Verifies the user can select favorite Pokémon up to the maximum and the UI prevents selecting more than 8.

steps.description: Click on eight different Pokémon in "Favorite Pokémon"
Click on a ninth Pokémon in "Favorite Pokémon"

title: Set featured team and save successfully
description: Verifies a user can select a featured team from available teams and save the profile with confirmation.

steps.description: Select the first available team from the "Featured team" dropdown

title: Copy public profile URL shows a visible copied confirmation
description: Verifies the user can use the Copy Public Profile URL control and receives a visible confirmation message.

steps.description: Verify element "Copy public profile URL" is visible

title: Username availability check recovers after switching from taken to available
description: Verifies the real-time username status updates correctly when changing from an unavailable username to an available one.

title: Admin user can open Admin Dashboard from Settings and see admin controls
description: Verifies an authenticated admin/owner can reach the Admin Dashboard and see admin controls rendered.

steps.description: Click on the "Admin" link or "Admin Dashboard" option

title: Admin Dashboard loads and displays warning about local-only actions (if shown)
description: Verifies the Admin Dashboard shows a visible warning that some actions may be local-only when applicable.

steps.description: Verify text "Some admin actions may be local-only" is visible

title: Admin user sees admin data section(s) load on the Admin Dashboard
description: Verifies that at least one admin data section is visible after the dashboard loads.

title: Non-admin user is blocked from viewing Admin Dashboard (restricted view)
description: Verifies an authenticated non-admin user is shown an access denied or restricted view on the Admin Dashboard route.

title: Non-admin restricted view does not expose admin controls
description: Verifies that when access is denied, admin control panels/buttons are not visible.

title: Admin performs a CRUD create action and sees new item appear in the UI
description: Verifies an admin can create an item via an admin control and the UI reflects the new item immediately.

steps.description: Type "Test Admin Item" into the name/title field
Verify text "Test Admin Item" is visible

title: Admin edits an existing item and sees the updated value in the UI
description: Verifies an admin can edit an item via admin controls and the updated value is visible after saving.

steps.description: Click the "Edit" button for the first listed item
Type "Updated Admin Item" into the name/title field
Verify text "Updated Admin Item" is visible

title: Admin CRUD action shows local-only warning when backend is not wired
description: Verifies that after performing an admin action, a visible warning indicates the action may not be persisted server-side.

steps.description: Click the "Save" button for any editable admin setting
Verify text "Some admin actions may be local-only" is visible
